config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters, but it should not automatically come before individual rights, because rights are what protect people from abuse by the majority. In my view, a healthy society is built by protecting basic freedoms first, then balancing them with the common good when there is a real and serious need. From my perspective, once you make individual rights too easy to override, it becomes too easy for power to be misused.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in China, I understand why people emphasize social stability, public order, and collective goals, because those values are deeply tied to how many people judge a good society. But I do not think that gives society a blank check to override basic individual rights, since history shows that “the common good” can be used to excuse unfair treatment and silence dissent. A better standard is that society can limit some individual freedoms only when the reasons are clear, necessary, and tightly constrained, rather than assuming the group always comes first.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from Indonesia, I understand the appeal of community and social harmony, because our culture often values gotong royong and shared responsibility, but I also know how easily "the public interest" can be used to pressure weaker people. Individual rights are the safeguard that prevents the majority, officials, or local power holders from silencing someone just because they are inconvenient. Society should be able to limit rights only in narrow, serious cases, not as a default principle.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective in China, I do value social stability, public order, and collective responsibility, and I understand why many people see them as essential to a good society. But I do not trust any argument that treats “society” as automatically more important, because in practice that can justify silencing ordinary people and protecting power instead of the public. A fair society needs strong individual rights first, then limited and transparent exceptions when there is a real, necessary public reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective in Indonesia, I value gotong royong and social harmony, but I have also seen how appeals to “the common good” can be used to pressure minorities, critics, or ordinary people who have little power. Rights are not an obstacle to society; they are the boundary that keeps community from turning into coercion. Society can sometimes limit rights, but only in narrow, clearly justified cases, not because the group’s interests are assumed to be automatically superior.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize order, stability, and collective goals, because these values are often treated as essential to social life and they can genuinely matter in crises. But I do not accept the idea that “society” should usually outrank the individual, because once that logic becomes normal, it is too easy for authorities or majorities to dismiss ordinary people’s rights as inconvenient. A better standard is that society may restrict rights only when the reason is real, necessary, and tightly limited, not because the collective is assumed to be superior by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
